patches.replace: must agree to within ±0.2% (the half-width of the implied interval)
patches.rationale: Curmudgeon ISS-1194: 'better than ~0.4%' is ambiguous directional phrasing. The constraint is bilateral — values must fall within a ±0.2% band. Restate as half-width interval for precision.

patches.replace: ±5° declination-extreme swing the cycle modulates (i.e., the lunar-standstill amplitude: major vs minor standstills at ~28.6°/~18.3°)
patches.rationale: Curmudgeon ISS-1204: Add inline gloss explaining what the ±5° declination swing refers to (lunar standstill amplitude) so readers unfamiliar with the Saros/nodal terminology understand the quantity.

Curmudgeon ISS-1188: Remove spurious 'return false' from onclick handler in WIN-058. The return false prevents default behavior unnecessarily on a non-anchor element; showTab() handles navigation directly.
Curmudgeon ISS-1188: Remove spurious 'return false' from onclick handler in WIN-062 (predictions tab reference).
Curmudgeon ISS-1188: Remove spurious 'return false' from second onclick handler in WIN-062 (timestamp tab reference).
